executive_summary.narrative_summary: Operations are growing faster than systems. Support is reactive, key workflows are manual, and tools are not reliably connected. Under a 20–30% growth scenario, response times, errors, and approval delays compound into revenue leakage and payroll inflation.

bottlenecks.title: Reactive Customer Support Bottleneck
causal_chain: Support bandwidth consumed by low-value work

stress_simulation.growth_20_percent: Backlog becomes persistent; response times exceed 48 hours; escalations spike.
stress_simulation.growth_30_percent: Refund rate rises; review score drops; support becomes the first operational failure point.
stress_simulation.founder_absence: Escalation decisions stall; SLA collapses; customers churn silently.
stress_simulation.system_failure: Order-status uncertainty triggers ticket surge and overwhelms manual triage.

root_cause_hierarchy.level_1_symptom: Slow response times and high ticket volume
root_cause_hierarchy.level_3_architecture_failure: No deflection/routing automation layer; weak system integrations
root_cause_hierarchy.level_4_governance_failure: No automation-first operating model for customer lifecycle

intervention_path.short_term: Deploy AI deflection + routing for repetitive tickets (order status, returns, FAQs).
intervention_path.mid_term: Integrate unified ticketing + self-service portal connected to order data.
intervention_path.structural_redesign: Shift to predictive support with lifecycle triggers, at-risk order detection, and escalation logic.

supporting_questions.client_answer: We reply within 24–48 hours when busy.
supporting_questions.ai_interpretation: Reactive workflow with no SLA protection.
supporting_questions.structural_implication: Backlog grows non-linearly as volume increases.

supporting_questions.client_answer: Customers email us mostly for order status, returns, refunds.
supporting_questions.structural_implication: Automation can reduce load without hiring.

supporting_questions.client_answer: Refunds increase when support is slow.
supporting_questions.structural_implication: Support is a revenue protection function, not a cost center.

stress_simulation.growth_20_percent: Manual reporting time increases; data mismatches increase; decisions become delayed.
stress_simulation.growth_30_percent: Ops meetings become 'data reconciliation' instead of execution; errors become recurring.
stress_simulation.founder_absence: No single source of truth; teams interpret KPIs differently; priorities drift.
stress_simulation.system_failure: One integration break causes reporting blind spots and operational surprises.

root_cause_hierarchy.level_1_symptom: Reports take too long; teams disagree on numbers
root_cause_hierarchy.level_2_process_failure: Data is stitched manually across tools
root_cause_hierarchy.level_3_architecture_failure: No unified data layer / event pipeline / standardized KPIs
root_cause_hierarchy.level_4_governance_failure: No defined reporting ownership + KPI definitions + data quality checks

intervention_path.short_term: Standardize KPIs + automate weekly dashboards from source systems.
intervention_path.mid_term: Introduce unified operational dashboard + scheduled data checks.
intervention_path.structural_redesign: Build a lightweight data layer and event-driven reporting for real-time visibility.

supporting_questions.client_answer: We build weekly reports in spreadsheets.
supporting_questions.ai_interpretation: Manual reporting indicates missing instrumentation or integration.
supporting_questions.structural_implication: Decision velocity slows as complexity grows.

supporting_questions.client_answer: Our tools don’t talk to each other consistently.
supporting_questions.structural_implication: Automation projects will fail without a data spine.

bottlenecks.title: Approval Bottleneck & Founder Dependency
causal_chain: Work queues stall waiting for decisions
Team speed depends on one person

stress_simulation.growth_20_percent: Approval queues grow; delays become normal; customers feel the lag.
stress_simulation.growth_30_percent: Founder becomes operational choke point; teams become reactive; quality drops.
stress_simulation.founder_absence: Work halts or proceeds inconsistently; rework increases.
stress_simulation.system_failure: No policy automation means exceptions escalate to humans by default.

root_cause_hierarchy.level_1_symptom: Work waits on approvals and escalations
root_cause_hierarchy.level_2_process_failure: No standardized rules for routing/approving requests
root_cause_hierarchy.level_3_architecture_failure: No workflow engine for approvals, SLAs, and exception handling
root_cause_hierarchy.level_4_governance_failure: Decision rights not distributed; no operational playbooks

intervention_path.short_term: Define decision rights + create approval SLAs and templates.
intervention_path.mid_term: Implement workflow routing and exception policies with human override.
intervention_path.structural_redesign: Operational playbooks + governance automation to remove dependency on any one person.

supporting_questions.structural_implication: Growth increases queue time and error rate.

systemic_patterns.failure_cascade_potential: Manual work consumes capacity and creates compounding delays under growth.

systemic_patterns.pattern_name: Fragmented Tool Stack Without Reliable Data Flow
systemic_patterns.failure_cascade_potential: Integration breaks create reporting blind spots and operational surprises.
Approvals become queues; execution velocity becomes person-dependent.

operations.interpretation: Operational throughput is constrained by manual workflows and inconsistent handoffs.

revenue.interpretation: Revenue engine works, but leaks occur when support and lifecycle systems lag.

systems.interpretation: Tool stack exists but lacks a reliable integration spine and unified reporting.

governance.interpretation: Decision rights and approvals are concentrated, slowing execution under load.

risk_model.cascade_path: Support delay → cancellations/refunds → negative reviews → higher CAC → lower margin

risk_model.trigger_threshold: Any integration outage or inconsistent tool syncing
risk_model.time_to_failure_estimate: Immediate visibility loss within 1–2 reporting cycles
risk_model.cascade_path: Bad data → wrong decisions → operational misalignment → recurring errors

risk_model.trigger_threshold: Founder unavailable or overloaded; approval queues increase
risk_model.cascade_path: Approvals stall → cycle time expands → team frustration → quality drops

risk_model.trigger_threshold: Handling sensitive customer data without formal policies
risk_model.time_to_failure_estimate: Policy risk increases as team size grows
risk_model.cascade_path: Data handling gaps → reputational risk → vendor/partner restrictions

answers.question: Describe your current operations workflow.
answers.text: We run day-to-day ops in Slack and spreadsheets; tasks are manual and assigned ad-hoc.

answers.question: How do you report on performance?
answers.text: Weekly spreadsheets; we pull data from Shopify and email reports manually.

answers.question: What is your average support response time?
Where do you lose the most time weekly?
Do your tools talk to each other?
answers.text: Not reliably. Some integrations break or aren’t set up fully.

answers.question: What are the most common customer requests?
answers.text: Order status, returns, refunds, and delivery questions.

answers.question: Where do approvals slow things down?
answers.text: Founder sign-off is needed for escalations and special cases.

answers.question: How do you handle inventory and fulfillment updates?
answers.text: Mostly manual checks; sometimes inventory mismatches happen.

answers.question: What happens when support is slow?
answers.text: Refunds rise and customers complain publicly.

answers.question: How do you follow up after purchase?
Do you use any AI today?
answers.text: Basic tools for writing, nothing operational.

answers.question: What’s your biggest growth constraint?
answers.text: Things break when volume spikes, we hire but it doesn’t fix the core problems.

answers.question: How do you manage exceptions?
answers.text: We escalate in Slack; decisions vary by person.

answers.question: What’s your timeline to improve operations?
